news-global-econ.title: Film producer says 'Made in America' movies can help revive Hollywood's struggling industry
news-global-econ.description: Hollywood executive Chris Fenton said studios became too reliant on China and now advocates for domestic production with his new film "Bad Counselors."

news-global-econ.title: How residents in this southern state are getting richer amid its population explosion
news-global-econ.description: Texas economy hits $2.9 trillion in 2025, with per-capita output rising to $91,500 per resident even as the state added nearly 419,000 new people.

news-global-econ.title: MORNING GLORY: The GOP’s choice in 2028 — more of MAGA or a reversion to the mean?
news-global-econ.description: Vice President J.D. Vance leads 2028 GOP presidential contenders, but DeSantis, Kemp, Youngkin, and Pompeo are expected to challenge in primaries.

news-global-econ.title: Either ignore climate change lunacy or high energy prices will make you wish you had
news-global-econ.description: Democrats target Trump on energy prices, but the climate agenda's regulations on coal, natural gas and oil made energy less affordable for Americans.

news-global-econ.title: Mass Migration Might Be a National Security Risk After All, Admits UK Government Terrorism Expert
news-global-econ.description:
Trump was right to say there is a national security risk posed by open borders, the British government's top terror expert belatedly admits.

news-global-soc.title: India fertilizer offers sink in sign of easing supply strain
news-global-soc.description: National Fertilizers, a state-run producer that also imports urea received offers between $444.90 and $617 a tonne, according to people familiar with the matter

news-global-soc.title: U.S. Hits Iran Again After Trump Said Tehran Has Been ‘Tapping Us Along’ in Talks
news-global-soc.description:
The U.S. military launched a fresh wave of strikes against multiple targets in Iran on Wednesday evening after President Donald Trump warned Tehran would “pay the price” for dragging out negotiations aimed at ending the conflict.

news-global-soc.title: Americans born after 1970 face higher death rates from several major causes in middle age
news-global-soc.description: Americans born after 1970 are dying faster than their parents, with higher rates of heart disease, cancer and overdoses between ages 30 and 49.

news-global-soc.title: How do teams advance in the 2026 World Cup? Tiebreaker rules and Round of 32 format explained
news-global-soc.description: The 2026 World Cup expands to 48 teams across the U.S., Canada, and Mexico, featuring new group stage tiebreakers and a Round of 32 knockout format.

news-global-soc.title: Iran Threatens More Bombings After U.S. Strikes: 'Leave If You Want to Be Safe'
news-global-soc.description: Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi warned in a message on Tuesday that American forces should leave the Middle East "if you want to be safe," threatening a response after the Pentagon confirmed pervasive airstrikes against Iranian military sites that day…
